$schema_note: Sprint brief for the Sanders Intelligence app. One file per micro-sprint (1 day cadence). Each task is a self-contained implementation prompt: outline, technical requirements, expected results, test coverage. Generated by the sprint-planner skill.
theme: Recently-added small wins + finish the vendor-profit thread

project.name: Sanders intelligence app
project.stack: React 18 + TypeScript + Vite, Tailwind, React Router v6, TanStack Query v5, Supabase, Recharts

selection_rationale: All three tasks were added on 2026-05-25 (recency boost) or are direct finishes of in-flight work. None require new external integrations, all fit a single day, and each has clear acceptance criteria. Sprint 1 of the post-profit-feature cycle.
instructions_for_implementer: Work tasks in the 'order' field. For EACH task: read 'clarifying_questions' first and ask the user before writing code. Treat 'likely_files' as starting points for investigation, not the full surface. Implement against 'requirements', validate against 'completion_criteria', and write/extend tests per 'test_coverage'. When done, update the matching Asana task's completion status and leave a comment citing the file/line evidence (mirrors the convention used when closing 1215004196463569).

tasks.title: Adjust data-freshness banner to include overnight mySQL pull timing
tasks.outline: The DataFreshnessBar at the top of every page currently shows only 'Data current as of <date>' or a stale/no-data variant. Users need to know not just the upload date, but also when the nightly mySQL pull last ran (or is expected to run next), so they can tell whether 'today's data' actually includes overnight movement.
tasks.technical_description: DataFreshnessBar (app/src/components/layout/DataFreshnessBar.tsx) is a 55-line component that runs a single Supabase query against the uploads table, computes a Freshness object (status + date), and renders one of three banners (fresh / stale / no_data). It does NOT currently surface any mySQL-pull-related timestamp. The fix is two-part: (a) decide where the mySQL pull timestamp lives (a new column on uploads, a row in a new system_status table, or a config row), and (b) extend fetchFreshness + the JSX to render that secondary timestamp inline with the existing 'Data current as of' line.
likely_files: supabase/migrations/ - new migration if a new column/table is required
requirements: Surface the most recent overnight mySQL pull timestamp (and pull status: success / failed / running) next to the existing 'Data current as of' text.
Format relative timing using the same fmtDate helper so the bar reads naturally, e.g. 'Data current as of 2026-05-25 - last overnight pull 2026-05-26 02:14 (success)'.
If the most recent pull is missing or older than 36 hours, downgrade the banner to the 'stale' variant even when the upload date is today.
Read the timestamp via the existing useQuery in fetchFreshness (single round-trip); do not introduce a second hook.
Do not change the banner DOM structure in a way that breaks the existing layout (height stays ~32px).
completion_criteria: When viewing any page as any role, the freshness bar shows both the upload date and the latest overnight mySQL pull timestamp.
If the overnight pull failed or has not run in >36h, the bar renders in the danger color regardless of upload date.
fmtDate is reused; no new date-formatting code is added.
No new console errors, no TypeScript errors, all existing tests pass.
expected_outcomes: Purchasing users can tell at a glance whether the data they see reflects overnight order activity, not just the last manual upload, which closes a long-standing source of 'why is this stale?' confusion.

unit: Add tests under app/src/__tests__/DataFreshnessBar.helpers.test.ts (extract any branching logic into a helper module first) covering: (a) fresh upload + recent pull = 'fresh', (b) fresh upload + stale pull = 'stale', (c) no upload but recent pull = 'no_data', (d) failed pull = 'stale' regardless of pull recency.
integration: Verify the bar renders both timestamps via a snapshot or RTL render test with a mocked supabase response.
manual_qa: Load each page (Action Center, Inventory Browser, Inbound Pipeline, Executive Summary, Tasks, Uploads, Vendor View) and confirm the banner shows both lines.
Manually update the mySQL pull timestamp in the DB to >36h ago and verify the banner downgrades to danger.

clarifying_questions: Where will the overnight mySQL pull timestamp be stored? (new uploads column, new system_status table, or pulled from a fixed-row config?)
Is the pull status tracked (success / failed / running), or only the timestamp?
Should the secondary text be inline ('upload date ... pull date') or a sub-line below the main message?
tasks.pre_implementation_instruction: Ask the clarifying questions and wait for explicit direction before editing files.

tasks.title: Vendor View - add Total Profit (30d) column and KPI card
tasks.outline: Vendor View already shows COGS% and Margin% per vendor (cogsPct30d, marginPct30d). What it still lacks is the absolute Total Profit per vendor, which is what purchasing actually ranks by when deciding who to push or pull back on. The profit number is already computed by buildVendorWindowMetrics; it just isn't surfaced as a top-level column or KPI.
tasks.technical_description: VendorView (app/src/pages/purchasing/VendorView.tsx) builds a VendorRow per supplier in a useMemo at line 94. Each VendorWindowMetrics object already contains a summed 'profit' field; the row exposes cogsPct30d and marginPct30d on line 116-117 but no profit. The table headers (line 261-275) and KPI strip (line 213-229) need a Total Profit (30d) column and a fifth KPI card. Margin% (line 274) is the structural twin to follow - same Sortable header, same nullable-sort wrapper, same fmtNullable display pattern.
likely_files: app/src/pages/purchasing/VendorView.tsx - VendorRow interface (around line 30-44), vendorRows builder (line 94-122), KPI strip (213-229), table header (261-275), table cells (lines following 307)
app/src/pages/purchasing/VendorView.helpers.ts - buildVendorWindowMetrics already sums profit per window (no change expected)
app/src/__tests__/VendorView.helpers.test.ts - add coverage for the new totalProfit30d field
requirements: Add totalProfit30d: number | null to the VendorRow interface. Set it to windowMetrics['30d'].profit when windowMetrics.hasMetrics is true, otherwise null.
Add a 'Total Profit' (or 'Total Profit (30d)' if confirmed) column to the vendor table with a SortableTh keyed on totalProfit30d, placed immediately after Margin %.
Cell display: fmtCurrency(value); negative values render in text-danger; null renders 'N/A' in text-text2.
Null/absent profit sorts last in both asc and desc directions - rely on the existing displayRows sort comparator which already nulls-last (lines 144-156).
Add a fifth KPI card to the strip: 'Total Profit (30d)' = sum of totalProfit30d across all vendor rows (treat nulls as 0). Update grid-cols-4 to grid-cols-5.
Update the empty-state colSpan from 14 to 15.
completion_criteria: The Vendor View table renders a Total Profit column for every vendor row with the 30-day accrual profit.
Sorting ascending and descending works; vendors with no metrics sort last in both directions.
The KPI strip shows a fifth 'Total Profit (30d)' card that matches the sum of the visible column values when no filter is applied.
Per-vendor totals reconcile with the per-window profit shown in the expanded vendor metrics panel.
No console errors, no TypeScript errors, all existing tests in app/src/__tests__/VendorView.helpers.test.ts still pass.
expected_outcomes: Purchasing can rank vendors by actual profit generated - completing the 'rank by profitability' workflow that COGS% and Margin% started.

unit: Extend app/src/__tests__/VendorView.helpers.test.ts: assert that when buildVendorWindowMetrics is called with a fixture set, the 30d.profit value equals the expected sum across the vendor's SKUs.
Add a test for the nullable-sort behavior: a list with [{totalProfit30d: 100}, {totalProfit30d: null}, {totalProfit30d: 50}] sorted desc must yield [100, 50, null].
integration: RTL render the VendorView with a fixture: assert the new column header is present and the KPI card shows the expected total.
manual_qa: Compare the new Total Profit value for one vendor to the per-window profit shown in the expanded metrics row - must match.
Sort the column asc/desc; confirm 'N/A' rows stay at the bottom.
Confirm KPI total recomputes when a category filter is applied.

clarifying_questions: Column label: 'Total Profit' or 'Total Profit (30d)' to make the window explicit?
Should the new KPI card be a fifth slot (grid-cols-5), or replace one of the existing four?
Negative profit: render in text-danger consistently with Margin %, or use a different treatment?
tasks.pre_implementation_instruction: Ask the clarifying questions and wait for explicit direction before editing files.

tasks.title: Auto-task creation based on logical gating - rule 1 (price review on COGS rise)
tasks.scope: medium (scoped to ONE gating rule for this sprint)
tasks.outline: Purchasing wants the app to auto-create review tasks when specific conditions are detected in newly uploaded data. The Asana task notes lists three rules: (1) selling price unchanged but COGS up = price review task, (2) status change = SKU review, (3) at-risk SKU% up 1% per vendor = vendor review. To keep this sprint to one day, implement ONLY rule 1 - the price review trigger - and structure the code so rules 2 and 3 can be added later by extending an array of gating rules.
tasks.technical_description: There is currently no automation pipeline; tasks are created exclusively through TaskModal. The Phase 2 backlog item (1214539638231261, due 2026-06-15) calls for a Supabase Edge Function to do this server-side, but for sprint 1 we want a client-side stop-gap: when a fresh upload completes (UploadsPage finishes its CSV ingest), diff the new inventory_records against the previous upload's, find SKUs where current.selling_price <= prior.selling_price + epsilon AND current.cost_price > prior.cost_price * 1.02 (configurable), and create tasks via useCreateTask with source='auto'. Each generated task should be vendor-grouped (one task per vendor, with affected SKUs in the description) to match the existing vendor-level task convention.
likely_files: app/src/pages/admin/UploadsPage.tsx - where uploads complete; the hook point for the trigger
app/src/hooks/useTasks.ts - useCreateTask exists; may need a useCreateAutoTasks variant that bulk-inserts
app/src/lib/autoTaskRules.ts - NEW: pure-function module exporting an array of GatingRule objects, each with { id, name, detect(prev, current): AffectedSku[], formatTask(affected): TaskDraft }
app/src/__tests__/autoTaskRules.test.ts - NEW: unit tests for the price-review rule
supabase/migrations/ - none required for rule 1 (the tasks table already has source='auto')
requirements: Create app/src/lib/autoTaskRules.ts. Export a GatingRule type, an array AUTO_TASK_RULES, and a runRules(prevRecords, currentRecords): TaskDraft[] function.
Implement the price-review rule as the first GatingRule entry: detect SKUs where selling_price is unchanged (within $0.01) and cost_price has risen by more than 2%.

Group affected SKUs by vendor; emit one TaskDraft per vendor with title='Price review: COGS rose without selling-price update', priority='high', sku_code = first affected SKU code, description listing every affected SKU + the old/new COGS values.

Wire runRules into UploadsPage's upload-complete flow: after the new inventory_records are inserted, fetch the previous upload's records, run rules, and insert any generated tasks via useCreateAutoTasks. Surface a non-blocking toast: 'N auto-tasks created'.
Skip auto-creation entirely if there is no previous upload (first ever load).
De-duplicate: if a task with the same auto-rule signature + vendor already exists in status='todo' for the current week, do not create a duplicate.
All auto-tasks must have source='auto' (already on the schema) and created_by = current admin user.
completion_criteria: Uploading a fresh CSV where vendor V has 3 SKUs with raised COGS and unchanged selling price creates exactly ONE 'Price review' task for vendor V, with all 3 SKUs listed in the description.
Uploading the SAME CSV again creates zero new tasks (dedupe works).
First-ever upload creates zero auto-tasks (no prior upload to diff against).
Toast confirms the number of tasks created.
autoTaskRules.ts has 100% statement coverage in its tests.
No console errors, no TypeScript errors, all existing tests pass.
expected_outcomes: Purchasing stops having to manually scan for COGS-vs-price drift; the system surfaces actionable price reviews automatically on upload. Lays the structural foundation (GatingRule array) so rules 2 and 3 are a one-file change next sprint.

unit: - price-review detects SKUs with COGS rise > 2% and selling price within $0.01
- price-review ignores SKUs where selling price changed
- price-review ignores SKUs where COGS rose by <= 2%
- runRules groups multiple affected SKUs under the same vendor into one TaskDraft
- runRules returns empty array when prevRecords is empty (first upload)
- dedupe filter removes a TaskDraft when an open task with the same auto-signature exists
integration: Mock supabase in UploadsPage tests: simulate an upload-complete event with a fixture diff and assert useCreateAutoTasks is called with the expected drafts.
manual_qa: Upload a fixture CSV with one vendor's COGS bumped 5% and selling price unchanged - confirm exactly one auto-task appears under that vendor in the Tasks page.
Re-upload the same CSV - no duplicate.
Upload a CSV where the selling price also moved - no auto-task generated.

clarifying_questions: Confirm the COGS-rise threshold (2%) and selling-price epsilon ($0.01). Should they live in autoTaskRules.ts constants or in a config row in the DB?
Should rules 2 and 3 (status-change SKU review, +1% at-risk vendor review) be added in this same sprint, or deferred to the Phase 2 Edge Function task (1214539638231261)?
When the toast fires, should it link directly to the Tasks page filtered by source='auto', or just show the count?
Department for auto-created tasks: always 'purchasing', or derived from the vendor?
tasks.pre_implementation_instruction: Ask the clarifying questions and wait for explicit direction before editing files. The threshold and the rules-2-and-3 scoping decision will materially change the size of this sprint.

sprint_close_checklist: All three tasks marked completed in Asana with a comment citing file paths and line numbers (mirror the 1215004196463569 close comment).
All new tests pass: cd app && npm test.
Run cd app && npm run dev and smoke-test the affected pages end-to-end.
Update CLAUDE.md if any new module (autoTaskRules.ts) or convention was added.
Update Memory: project_sanders_intelligence.md if the upload pipeline now has an auto-task side effect.
